claim: AI projects fail at scale primarily due to weak data foundations, not model limitations; successful AI requires treating data engineering as critical infrastructure before building agents
actionable: Before deploying AI agents, invest in unified data ingestion, cataloging, and governance; data engineering foundations directly determine agent reliability and success
source_title: Data Engineering from Ingestion to AI-Ready | BUILD 2025 Keynote

claim: Snowpipe Streaming V2 achieves 10-second data latency with 50% cost reduction through architectural optimization; enables near-real-time data availability for AI agents (demonstrated with CBOE processing 190 billion rows daily)
actionable: When implementing streaming data pipelines for AI, target sub-10-second latency and evaluate cost-per-GB pricing models; modern streaming architectures can deliver real-time data cost-effectively
source_title: Data Engineering from Ingestion to AI-Ready | BUILD 2025 Keynote

claim: OpenFlow (Snowflake's data integration tool) with 26+ managed connectors eliminates the need for custom ETL code; Snowflake-hosted deployment removes infrastructure management burden for data teams
actionable: For data ingestion at enterprise scale, leverage managed connectors (Salesforce, Oracle, Workday, SAP) that provide zero-ETL integration rather than building custom pipelines; prioritize fully-hosted deployment options
source_title: Data Engineering from Ingestion to AI-Ready | BUILD 2025 Keynote

claim: Dynamic tables (declarative SQL-based pipelines) with incremental refreshes eliminate orchestration overhead; Snowflake manages table updates automatically, enabling non-engineers to build data pipelines
actionable: Use declarative pipeline definitions (dynamic tables) instead of imperative ETL code; let platforms handle incremental updates and scheduling automatically
source_title: Data Engineering from Ingestion to AI-Ready | BUILD 2025 Keynote

claim: Snowpark Connect for Apache Spark achieves 8x faster execution and 3x lower cost than managed Spark services; allows running unmodified Spark code on Snowflake without infrastructure management
actionable: For existing Spark workloads, migrate to Snowflake's Spark integration to gain significant performance and cost benefits without rewriting code; reduces operational overhead of maintaining separate Spark clusters
source_title: Data Engineering from Ingestion to AI-Ready | BUILD 2025 Keynote

claim: AI SQL functions (AI Extract, AI Redact) reduce manual data processing tasks from hours to single SQL statements; enables data engineers to handle sensitive data masking and extraction programmatically
actionable: Leverage AI-powered SQL functions for data transformation tasks (extraction, redaction, anonymization); eliminates manual data manipulation and reduces task time significantly
source_title: Data Engineering from Ingestion to AI-Ready | BUILD 2025 Keynote

claim: Horizon Catalog with embedded Apache Polaris/Iceberg REST APIs enables multi-engine access to data without separate account management; supports both Snowflake and external query engines in single governance layer
actionable: Use unified catalogs (Horizon) to centralize governance across Snowflake and open table formats (Iceberg); avoid managing separate access control systems for different engines
source_title: Data Engineering from Ingestion to AI-Ready | BUILD 2025 Keynote

claim: Data metric functions (DMF) enable monitoring of data quality (referential integrity, null counts, min/max values) with automated alerting; failed checks can trigger alerts and programmatic remediation
actionable: Implement data quality monitoring with DMF and create alerts for quality violations; tie alerts to remediation workflows or incident escalation
source_title: Data Engineering from Ingestion to AI-Ready | BUILD 2025 Keynote

claim: Data teams that invested in modernization (migration from legacy warehouses to unified data clouds) can deploy AI agents faster; example: Zealus deployed 20+ agents in weeks because their data foundation was already unified
actionable: Invest in data modernization and unification as a prerequisite for rapid AI agent deployment; teams with clean, centralized data can iterate 10x faster on agent development
source_title: Data Engineering from Ingestion to AI-Ready | BUILD 2025 Keynote

claim: Cortex Code (AI-powered code assistant in Snowflake IDE) generates SQL and governance code from natural language; reduces time spent remembering syntax and complex access control patterns
actionable: Use AI-powered code generation for boilerplate SQL, access controls (RBAC/ABAC), and alerts; focus engineering time on logic and data modeling rather than syntax
source_title: Data Engineering from Ingestion to AI-Ready | BUILD 2025 Keynote
